deep_dive.title: GC-LoRA: Gated Convolutional LoRA for Parameter-Efficient Acoustic Adaptation
deep_dive.abstract: Transformer-based Speech Foundation Models excel in most Automatic Speech Recognition tasks but often suffer performance degradation when applied to domains with mismatched acoustic characteristics. While Parameter Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) methods, such as Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), adjust global attention, they lack the local context modeling crucial for capturing domain-specific variations. We propose GC-LoRA, a novel adapter architecture that injects Conformer-style local convolutional processing into pretrained Transformer encoders. By integrating a lightweight adapter to encoder attention output projections, our method efficiently captures local acoustic dependencies without disrupting pretrained global representations. Experiments across diverse datasets (acoustically-degraded, bandlimited, dialectal, child) demonstrate the efficacy of our approach, achieving Word Error Rate (WER) reductions of up to 10.9% compared to baselines while adding minimal trainable parameters.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning method with gated convolutional adapter
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于门控卷积的低秩适配参数高效微调方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于门控卷积的低秩适配参数高效微调方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于门控卷积的低秩适配参数高效微调方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes GC-LoRA, an adapter that injects local convolutional processing into pretrained Transformer encoders for efficient acoustic adaptation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出GC-LoRA，通过将Conformer式卷积注入预训练Transformer编码器的注意力输出投影，实现了参数高效的声学适应。在多个ASR基准上优于标准LoRA，表明局部卷积适配能有效提升域适应性能。

deep_dive.title: Implicit Neural Representations of Individual Behavior
deep_dive.abstract: We study policy representation learning from unlabeled multi-policy behavioral data. Each episode is generated by a fixed policy, but policy labels are unavailable. This setting appears in robotics play, demonstrations, games, racing, and other datasets where heterogeneous behaviors are mixed without annotations. We introduce \emph{Behavioral INR}, a self-supervised generative model that adapts implicit neural representations (INRs) from vision to behavior. Instead of mapping coordinates to RGB values, Behavioral INR represents a policy as a state-action function mapping states to subsequent actions. An episode-level latent modulates this function through FiLM layers, yielding a generative prior over policies and allowing policy identity to be inferred without supervision. Because INRs treat each datapoint as samples from an underlying function, the same model naturally accommodates variable episode lengths and different sampling granularities, as in vision INRs with different image resolutions. We also define policy-level out-of-distribution (OOD) shifts along state-distribution and action-distribution axes, which arise when policies overlap in states or actions but are not captured by standard behavioral OOD settings based only on new agents or environments. We evaluate on synthetic Gaussian random field data, MuJoCo demonstrations with controlled OOD splits, and real-world chess, Formula 1 racing, robotics, and Seek-Avoid datasets. Behavioral INR most consistently improves policy identifiability in the hardest continuous state-action settings, especially when longer episodes, more policies, and OOD splits reduce the usefulness of marginal shortcuts; amortized history encoders remain competitive when policy identity can be recovered from symbolic repetition or low-dimensional action statistics. We release code and checkpoints.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: self-supervised generative model for policy representation learning using implicit neural representations
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自我监督隐式神经表征用于策略表示学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自我监督隐式神经表征用于策略表示学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自我监督隐式神经表征用于策略表示学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Behavioral INR, a self-supervised generative model that uses implicit neural representations to learn policy representations from unlabeled multi-policy behavioral data, enabling policy identity inference without supervision.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究了从无标签多策略行为数据中学习策略表征的问题。提出Behavioral INR，一种自我监督生成模型，将隐式神经表征（INR）从视觉领域迁移到行为领域，将策略表示为状态-动作函数，并通过FiLM层用片段级隐变量调控。该模型无需策略标签即可推断策略身份，为机器人演示、游戏等场景的策略学习提供了高效的自监督表示方法。

quick_skim.title: Learning to Adapt: Representation-Based Reinforcement Learning for Multi-Task Skill Transfer
quick_skim.abstract: Reinforcement learning has achieved remarkable success in learning complex control policies, yet its applicability remains limited due to sample inefficiency and poor generalization across tasks. In this work, we propose RepMT-SAC, a framework for multi-task RL that enables efficient knowledge sharing and robust transfer to new tasks. RepMT-SAC uses spectral MDP decomposition to capture transferable dynamics, structuring the value function into a task-agnostic core with a minimal task-specific adjustment. This design allows for strong zero-shot performance on in-distribution tasks and rapid few-shot adaptation to out-of-distribution tasks. We evaluate RepMT-SAC on quadcopter trajectory-following tasks across in-distribution and out-of-distribution contexts, demonstrating that it outperforms baselines by up to 30%.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Representation-based reinforcement learning for multi-task skill transfer via spectral MDP decomposition
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于表示的强化学习通过谱MDP分解实现多任务技能迁移
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于表示的强化学习通过谱MDP分解实现多任务技能迁移
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于表示的强化学习通过谱MDP分解实现多任务技能迁移
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes RepMT-SAC, a multi-task RL framework using spectral MDP decomposition to learn transferable representations, enabling zero-shot and few-shot adaptation across tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 强化学习样本效率低且跨任务泛化差。提出RepMT-SAC框架，利用谱MDP分解捕获可迁移动态，将值函数分为任务无关核心与最小任务特定调整。在四旋翼轨迹跟踪任务上，对分布内任务实现强零样本性能，对分布外任务快速少样本适应。展示了表示学习在强化学习迁移中的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 强化学习样本效率低且跨任务泛化差。提出RepMT-SAC框架，利用谱MDP分解捕获可迁移动态，将值函数分为任务无关核心与最小任务特定调整。在四旋翼轨迹跟踪任务上，对分布内任务实现强零样本性能，对分布外任务快速少样本适应。展示了表示学习在强化学习迁移中的有效性。

quick_skim.title: S$^2$COPE: Self-Supervised Concept Discovery via Preference Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Current representation learning paradigms force a fundamental compromise: self-supervised methods scale to massive datasets but yield opaque features, whereas interpretable models remain bottlenecked by the need for dense human annotation. We introduce Self-Supervised Concept discOvery via Preference lEarning (\model), a label-free framework that resolves this dilemma. Instead of treating Vision-Large-Language Models (VLLMs) as static feature extractors, \model leverages them as active participants in a self-supervised preference optimization loop. By autonomously hypothesizing, validating, and reinforcing candidate visual attributes directly from raw imagery, our framework discovers novel, structured concepts without a single label. Extensive experiments across natural, medical, and physics domains demonstrate that \model successfully extracts domain-specific concepts where standard VLLMs often fail to generate. By amortizing concept discovery directly into the VLLM backbone through our self-supervised preference objective -- rather than relying on static generation and disjoint filtering -- we achieve up to a 24-point absolute improvement in downstream top-1 classification accuracy on unseen data. Our work suggest that interpretability can emerge through a model's autonomous interaction with incidental visual structures, without any human supervision.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: self-supervised concept discovery via preference learning improves representation learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过偏好学习进行自监督概念发现，提升表征学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过偏好学习进行自监督概念发现，提升表征学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过偏好学习进行自监督概念发现，提升表征学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: S2COPE discovers structured visual concepts without labels by using VLLMs as active participants in a self-supervised preference optimization loop.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有自监督方法虽可扩展但特征不透明，而可解释模型依赖密集标注。S2COPE利用视觉大语言模型在自监督偏好优化循环中自主假设、验证和强化候选视觉属性，无需任何标签即可发现新颖、结构化的概念。实验表明，该框架在概念发现和下游任务中表现优异，弥合了自监督与可解释性之间的鸿沟。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有自监督方法虽可扩展但特征不透明，而可解释模型依赖密集标注。S2COPE利用视觉大语言模型在自监督偏好优化循环中自主假设、验证和强化候选视觉属性，无需任何标签即可发现新颖、结构化的概念。实验表明，该框架在概念发现和下游任务中表现优异，弥合了自监督与可解释性之间的鸿沟。
